en.value: The project aims to support the following number of people of the poor urban settlements based on the poverty mapping report and field assessment of Kamalamai and Itahari Municipalities:A total of 5 settlements in wards 11 and 12 with population of 3193 (623 households(HHs)) where more than 65% people are below the poverty line;A total of 17 settlements of urban poor and squatter communities in four different wards with population of 911HHs. The major activities are-Based on the preparation and assessment of water, environmental sanitation and hygiene improvement plan, provide safe water, affordable adequate sanitation and hygiene education to the targeted people of Kamalamai and Itahari Municipalities;Reduce the burden of collecting water, ensure equity of distribution, increase optimization of water use and reduce water borne diseases as well as increase dignity;Increase the capacity of civil society groups and 2 municipalities to work together to effectively implement water and sanitation improvement plans and promote environmental sanitation;Based on the lessons learnt, contribute in effective engagement and influencing municipal and central level urban water and sanitation programming and monitoring activities.

executing_agency: Ministry of Physical Infrastructure and Transport

en.full: Integrating Water and Sanitation Services to Urban Poor Communities in two Municipalities of Nepal

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
